recent_news.title: Wooptix Targets AI Packaging Bottleneck with Astronomy Tech
recent_news.summary: A Canary Islands startup brings wavefront imaging to semiconductor metrology, aiming to tackle wafer shape challenges in 3D integration. The post Wooptix Targets AI Packaging Bottleneck with Astronomy Tech appeared first on EE Times.
recent_news.body_text:
As chipmakers race to build faster AI processors, attention has shifted beyond transistor scaling and toward advanced packaging, hybrid bonding, and 3D integration. That shift is creating new manufacturing bottlenecks—among them, how to precisely measure wafer shape, topography, and warpage as devices become thinner, denser, and increasingly stacked.

recent_news.title: How Spain’s ICFO Helped Build a Quantum Security Startup for the AI Era
recent_news.summary: ICFO spinoff Quside has developed a quantum random number generator chip for secure cryptography applications. The post How Spain’s ICFO Helped Build a Quantum Security Startup for the AI Era appeared first on EE Times.
recent_news.body_text: In Barcelona, Quside is using photonic chip technology to generate what it calls high-quality, verifiable entropy—the unpredictable input used to create encryption keys, secure communications, authentication credentials, and other cryptographic functions. recent_news.title: Why shoplifting is bad
recent_news.summary: It may feel like a small act of rebellion, but it hurts a lot of people who don't deserve it.
recent_news.body_text:
“Shoplifters of the world/ Unite and take over” — The Smiths
“When she wants something, man, she don’t wanna pay for it” — Jane’s Addiction
Seven years ago, when I wanted some toothpaste, I would walk down to my local Walgreens, grab a box of Crest off of the shelf, pay for it at the register, and walk home with it. Today, when I want some toothpaste, I open up Amazon.com and buy it in bulk. What

recent_news.title: Why Chip Sovereignty Is No Longer About Chips—But Systems
recent_news.summary: Exclusive interviews with Mark Papermaster, CTO of AMD, and Chris Miller, author of “Chip War” and professor at Tufts University The post Why Chip Sovereignty Is No Longer About Chips—But Systems appeared first on EE Times.
recent_news.body_text:
Governments around the world are pouring tens of billions into semiconductor manufacturing in the name of chip sovereignty. From the U.S. to Europe to Asia, the assumption is clear: Control the fabs, and you control the future of computing.
But as AI reshapes the semiconductor landscape, that assumption no longer captures where value is created. Strategic advantage is shifting toward a broader—and

recent_news.title: Which Chips Actually Matter? Europe Reassesses Its Semiconductor Strategy After Nexperia Wakeup Call
recent_news.summary: As the Nexperia episode sharpens Europe’s focus, experts say resilience will hinge on application relevance, supply chain depth, and smarter investment priorities. The post Which Chips Actually Matter? Europe Reassesses Its Semiconductor Strategy After Nexperia Wakeup Call appeared first on EE Times.
recent_news.body_text:
The political shockwaves from the Nexperia episode are still reverberating across Europe’s semiconductor debate. What began as a company-specific controversy has evolved into a broader strategic question: Is Europe focusing on the right parts of the chip value chain—or chasing technological prestige at the expense of practical resilience?

recent_news.title: From Defect Images to Die Prediction: How Intel Is Scaling AI in Advanced Manufacturing
recent_news.summary: Intel’s AI strategy focuses on operational scale, lifecycle management, and factory-wide deployment. The post From Defect Images to Die Prediction: How Intel Is Scaling AI in Advanced Manufacturing appeared first on EE Times.
recent_news.body_text: Artificial intelligence has long been embedded in semiconductor manufacturing, quietly supporting inspection tools, statistical process control, and yield analysis. What is changing is not the mere presence of AI but its operational role. As device architectures grow more complex and process windows tighten, AI is increasingly becoming part of the core infrastructure that keeps advanced fabs runni

recent_news.title: Europe’s Semiconductor Strategy Meets Market Reality
recent_news.summary: As Brussels sharpens its de-risking agenda, analysts warn that the real test of Europe’s chip ambitions may come from market economics, not geopolitics. The post Europe’s Semiconductor Strategy Meets Market Reality appeared first on EE Times.
recent_news.body_text:
Europe’s semiconductor strategy has largely been framed as a geopolitical balancing act. Policymakers in Brussels speak of “de-risking, not decoupling,” aiming to reduce strategic dependencies without dismantling the global openness that built the modern chip industry.
But a more consequential stress test is emerging—one driven less by export controls and more by market economics.
For Antonia Hmai

recent_news.title: Plentiful, high-paying jobs in the age of AI
recent_news.summary: A timely repost, with some needed clarifications.
recent_news.body_text:
I’m traveling today, so here’s a timely repost.
Two years ago, I wrote a post on AI and jobs that ignited a firestorm of discussion and criticism:
